questions.question: Derive the distance formula using coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: The distance formula is derived using the Pythagoras theorem. By drawing perpendiculars from the given points to the axes, the distance between the points is obtained as √[(x₂ − x₁)² + (y₂ − y₁)²].

questions.question: Explain how to check whether three given points are collinear.
questions.answer: Three points are collinear if the sum of the distances between any two pairs of points is equal to the distance between the remaining pair.

questions.question: Explain the section formula for internal division.
questions.answer: The section formula gives the coordinates of a point dividing a line segment internally in a given ratio using proportional distances.

questions.question: Derive the midpoint formula using the section formula.
questions.answer: The midpoint divides the line segment in the ratio 1:1. Substituting m₁ = m₂ = 1 in the section formula gives the midpoint formula.

questions.question: Explain how coordinate geometry helps in finding the shape of a quadrilateral.
questions.answer: By using distance formula and midpoint formula, properties such as equal sides and equal diagonals can be verified.

questions.question: Explain how distance formula is used to verify a right-angled triangle.
questions.answer: By calculating all three sides and checking whether the square of the largest side equals the sum of squares of the other two sides.

questions.question: Explain the concept of locus with an example.
questions.answer: A locus is the set of all points satisfying a given condition. For example, points equidistant from two fixed points lie on the perpendicular bisector.

questions.question: Explain how midpoint formula is used to verify a parallelogram.
questions.answer: If the midpoints of diagonals are the same, the quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

questions.question: Explain how the distance formula helps in identifying a square.
questions.answer: A square has equal sides and equal diagonals which can be verified using distance formula.

questions.question: Explain internal division with a diagrammatic idea.
questions.answer: Internal division means the dividing point lies between two endpoints of the line segment.

questions.question: Explain why distance between two points is always positive.
questions.answer: Distance represents length, which cannot be negative.

questions.question: Explain how section formula is useful in real-life situations.
questions.answer: It helps locate positions such as relay towers or division points in given ratios.

questions.question: Explain why points on x-axis have zero y-coordinate.
questions.answer: Points on x-axis have no vertical distance from it, so y = 0.

questions.question: Explain why points on y-axis have zero x-coordinate.
questions.answer: Points on y-axis have no horizontal distance from it, so x = 0.

questions.question: Explain how distance formula helps in finding the perimeter of a triangle.
questions.answer: By calculating the lengths of all three sides using distance formula and adding them.

questions.question: Explain the importance of coordinate geometry in daily life.
questions.answer: It is used in navigation, engineering, mapping and construction.

questions.question: Explain how a triangle can be identified as isosceles using coordinates.
questions.answer: If two sides calculated using distance formula are equal, the triangle is isosceles.

questions.question: Explain the role of perpendicular axes in coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: They provide a reference system to locate points accurately.

questions.question: Explain how coordinates help in representing geometric figures.
questions.answer: Coordinates convert geometric figures into algebraic forms for easy analysis.

questions.question: Explain the meaning of an ordered pair.
questions.answer: An ordered pair (x, y) represents the position of a point in a plane.

questions.question: Explain how section formula is derived using similarity of triangles.
questions.answer: It is derived using AA similarity and proportional sides.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between algebra and coordinate geometry.
questions.answer: Algebraic equations are used to study geometric figures.

questions.question: Explain how midpoint formula helps in finding centres of figures.
questions.answer: It helps find the centre by locating the midpoint of diagonals.

questions.question: Explain the use of distance formula in verifying congruency.
questions.answer: Equal corresponding side lengths confirm congruency.

questions.question: Summarise the applications of distance and section formula.
questions.answer: They are used to find lengths, midpoints, ratios and verify geometric shapes.
